title: Resinous Greek Pear Tiropita
description: A resinous, bouncy-qq Greek tiropita featuring roasted Korean pear in a gluten-free phyllo crust, cooked in an iron teppan.
instructions: Preheat an iron teppan on medium-high heat for 5 minutes until hot.
Whisk the feta, ricotta, a pinch of salt, pepper, lemon juice, ground cinnamon, and a tablespoon of honey until smooth.
Stir in the toasted pine nuts and diced Korean pear to the cheese mixture.
Brush the hot teppan with a tablespoon of olive oil, then lay one sheet of gluten-free phyllo over the pan, letting it sizzle slightly.
Spread one third of the cheese-pear filling over the phyllo, then cover with another sheet of phyllo and repeat until all filling is used.
Fold the edges of the phyllo over the filling to seal the tiropita and lightly press down to create a compact shape.
Reduce heat to medium and cook the tiropita for 12 minutes, flipping midway to ensure even browning.
Once golden and bubbly, remove the tiropita from the teppan and let it rest for 4 minutes.
Slice the tiropita into wedges and drizzle additional honey for a resinous glaze.
Serve hot as a monsoon-inspired Greek dessert or snack.
